question: When was x402 on Stellar announced, what did that announcement mean, and what SEP number does x402 have?

golden.answer: SDF announced x402 on Stellar on March 10, 2026. x402 uses HTTP 402 Payment Required for programmatic per-request payments; Stellar's documented flow uses Soroban authorization and SEP-41-compatible tokens. At announcement, the settlement layer was live, while the agent tooling and MCP integration were still being built or in active development. x402 was not itself a Stellar Ecosystem Proposal and had no SEP number as of July 11, 2026; SEP-41 is the token interface, not ‘SEP-402.’
keyFacts: SDF's x402-on-Stellar announcement date was March 10, 2026.
x402 enables programmatic per-request HTTP payments on Stellar using the documented Soroban/token flow.
As of July 11, 2026, x402 was not a SEP and had no SEP number.
At announcement, settlement was live but agent/MCP tooling was still being built.
avoid: Do NOT invent SEP-402 or any other SEP number for x402.
Do NOT say SEP-41 is the x402 protocol specification; it is the token interface used by Stellar's implementation.
Do NOT imply every x402 agent or MCP integration was production-live on March 10, 2026.
golden.notes: The no-SEP statement is explicitly as-of and was checked against the official ecosystem proposal directory plus a web sweep.

corroboration.claim: SDF announced x402 on Stellar on 2026-03-10

evidence.note: Official implementation repository was created in the same launch window and documents Stellar x402 tooling.

corroboration.claim: x402 had no SEP number as of 2026-07-11

evidence.note: Official SEP directory inventory contains no x402 proposal.
Explicit general-web absence sweep found implementation/docs references but no SEP identity.

corroboration.claim: At the March 10 announcement, the settlement layer was live while agent tooling and MCP integration remained in active development.

evidence.note: Repository contains the agent-facing tools and examples; the official announcement supplies the live-settlement versus active-development status boundary.
